en.name: Z-M LR-300 5.56x45 assault rifle
en.description: The Z-M LR-300 is an American select-fire assault rifle designed by gunsmith Allan Zitta and manufactured by Z-M Weapons. The model name LR-300 stands for Light Rifle and 300 is for 300 meters, which is regarded by the manufacturer as the effective range of the rifle with a standard 55 gr (3.6 g) FMJ bullet. The design is based on the AR-15, M16 and C7 rifles, but has a unique semi-direct gas impingement system and a folding stock option.

en.name: Z-M LR-300 5.56x45 assault rifle FDE
en.description: The Z-M LR-300 is an American select-fire assault rifle designed by gunsmith Allan Zitta and manufactured by Z-M Weapons. The model name LR-300 stands for Light Rifle and 300 is for 300 meters, which is regarded by the manufacturer as the effective range of the rifle with a standard 55 gr (3.6 g) FMJ bullet. The design is based on the AR-15, M16 and C7 rifles, but has a unique semi-direct gas impingement system and a folding stock option.

en.name: Z-M LR-300 Charging Handle
en.description: A standard charging handle for the Z-M LR-300. The charging handle is proprietary and has a beefier design to fit the op-rod.

en.name: Z-M LR-300 Upper Receiver
en.description: To accommodate the unique forward buffer and gas impingement system, the proprietary upper is taller and thicker than a standard AR upper. The handguard rail has a removable top cover to access the recoil spring system for disassembly.

en.name: Z-M LR-300 Pistol Grip
en.description: The LR-300 polymer pistol grip can be installed on any weapon compatible with AR-15 grips.

en.name: Z-M LR-300 5.56x45 10.7 inch barrel
en.description: A 10.7 inch (271mm) barrel for LR-300 platform weapons for 5.56x45 NATO ammo. Corresponds with standard service M4 CQBR barrel.

en.name: Z-M LR-300 5.56x45 13.7 inch barrel
en.description: A 13.7 inch (348mm) barrel for LR-300 platform weapons for 5.56x45 NATO ammo.

en.name: Z-M LR-300 5.56x45 Gas Block
en.description: The LR-300 uses a modified version of the AR-15's standard direct impingement gas operating system dubbed delayed impingement gas system.[4] The gas key is extended beyond its normal length (manufacturer calls it an 'operating rod'), extending into the handguard. The gas key contains the return spring which is fixed between the front of the receiver and the collar at the front of the gas key. The gas port is under the back side of the gas block, gasses go up then forward to the front side of the gas block then up and back to the gas tube. The gas tube is about half of the length of a standard AR-15 tube. The gas tube is always inside of the gas key (minimal overlap is about 1 inch). This leaves the area near the bolt much cleaner. Almost all gasses exit the gun through the holes on the right side of the bolt carrier, and minimal leaks from the gas key to the handguard area do not hinder the gun's function.

en.name: Z-M LR-300 Handguard
en.description: A carbine length handguard manufactured by Z-M Weapons, a standard-issue handguard for the Z-M LR-300

en.name: Z-M LR-300 Stock Adapter
en.description: An adapter for installing stocks on the Z-M LR-300. Manufactured by Z-M Weapons

en.name: Z-M LR-300 Standard Stock
en.description: Contrary to the original AR-15 rifle design, the LR-300 has the capability to use a fully folding and adjustable skeletal stock. Most AR-15 rifle variants have a thick, cylindrical recoil buffer tube, more properly called the receiver extension, that protrudes approximately 20 cm (7.9 in) straight back from the rear of the receiver, and the firearm cannot operate without it. This normally precludes the use of anything but fixed or telescoping stocks. The LR-300 however uses a forward-mounted return spring which removes the need for a receiver extension.

en.name: Z-M LR-300 Light Stock
en.description: Contrary to the original AR-15 rifle design, the LR-300 has the capability to use a fully folding and adjustable skeletal stock. Most AR-15 rifle variants have a thick, cylindrical recoil buffer tube, more properly called the receiver extension, that protrudes approximately 20 cm (7.9 in) straight back from the rear of the receiver, and the firearm cannot operate without it. This normally precludes the use of anything but fixed or telescoping stocks. The LR-300 however uses a forward-mounted return spring which removes the need for a receiver extension.

en.name: Cut A2 Pistol Grip
en.description: A cut A2 pistol grip turned into a foregrip

en.name: Z-M LR-300 Rear Sight
en.description: The Z-M LR-300 detachable rear sight. Standard-issue for LR-300 assault rifles.

en.name: Z-M LR-300 5.56x45 flash hider
en.description: A standard-issue 5.56x45 flash hider for the Z-M LR-300 assault rifle
